---
title: "ECON 2B03: Introduction to Data Analysis and Statistics"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    toc-depth: 2
    link-external-newwindow: true
    link-external-icon: false
---

This is the course website for ECON2B03. I only update it when Iâ€™m teaching the course.

- [Course schedule (PDF)](schedule.pdf)
- [Course information (PDF)](course-info.pdf)

Below are the slides and notes, organized by part.

::: {.slides-table}
|  |  |
| --- | :--: |
| **Part A: Descriptive Statistics** |  |
| – A01: Principles of Data | [slides](slides/A_Descriptive_statistics/A01.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – A02: Data | [slides](slides/A_Descriptive_statistics/A02.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – A03: Categorical data | [slides](slides/A_Descriptive_statistics/A03.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – A04: Numerical data (1) | [slides](slides/A_Descriptive_statistics/A04.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – A05: Numerical data (2) | [slides](slides/A_Descriptive_statistics/A05.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – A06: Numerical data (3) | [slides](slides/A_Descriptive_statistics/A06.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| **Part B: Probability Theory** |  |
| – B01: Probability Concepts | [slides](slides/B_Probability_theory/B01.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – B02: Conditional Probability | [slides](slides/B_Probability_theory/B02.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – B03: Discrete Random Variables | [slides](slides/B_Probability_theory/B03.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – B04: The binomial distribution | [slides](slides/B_Probability_theory/B04.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – B05: Continuous random variables | [slides](slides/B_Probability_theory/B05.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – B06: Normal distribution | [slides](slides/B_Probability_theory/B06.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – B07: Normal tail areas | [slides](slides/B_Probability_theory/B07.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – B08: Sampling | [slides](slides/B_Probability_theory/B08.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – B09: The central limit theorem | [slides](slides/B_Probability_theory/B09.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| **Part C: Inferential Statistics** |  |
| – C01: Confidence intervals | [slides](slides/C_Inferential_Statistics/C01.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – C02: Confidence intervals: means and proportions | [slides](slides/C_Inferential_Statistics/C02.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – C03: Introduction to hypothesis testing | [slides](slides/C_Inferential_Statistics/C03.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – C04: The critical value approach | [slides](slides/C_Inferential_Statistics/C04.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – C05: The p-value approach | [slides](slides/C_Inferential_Statistics/C05.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – C06: Inference in two-population problems | [slides](slides/C_Inferential_Statistics/C06.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| **Part D: Linear Regression** |  |
| – D01: Correlation and visual association | [slides](slides/D_Linear_regression/D01.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – D02: Fitting the simple regression line | [slides](slides/D_Linear_regression/D02.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – D03: Inference for simple linear regression | [slides](slides/D_Linear_regression/D03.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – D04: Multiple linear regression | [slides](slides/D_Linear_regression/D04.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
| – D05: Prediction and the bias-variance tradeoff | [slides](slides/D_Linear_regression/D05.html){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} |
:::
